title: Add a small direct-AUROC optimization expert and blend by rank
summary: Current base models optimize logloss while the competition scores ROC AUC.
rationale: Current base models optimize logloss while the competition scores ROC AUC. Deep AUC-maximization work and LibAUC suggest a model trained on an AUC surrogate can produce a meaningfully different ranking, especially on imbalanced boundary samples.
implementation_hint: Train a compact MLP or linear-tabular expert on the existing engineered features using a pairwise/deep AUC loss, ideally on a candidate subset near likely stop windows to control compute. Use it only as a low-weight rank-blend component with XGB/CatBoost rather than replacing them.
expected_effect: Best case is not a standalone gain but a low-correlation reranker that nudges ambiguous positives upward and improves ensemble AUC.
risk: Standalone model may underperform trees; pairwise AUC losses can be noisy and need careful batching, regularization, and early stopping.
